title: Extended Producer Responsibility: The Law That Is Going to Change Everything for Plastic
excerpt: Extended Producer Responsibility is the legislation that forces the companies who make plastic to pay for what happens to it at end of life. It is already live in the EU. It is coming to Tanzania. And it makes the timing of MlimaLoop's build more important than most people realise.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: Who Is Getting Plastic Right. Who Is Years Behind. And Who Does Not Seem to Care.
excerpt: Some countries have cracked the plastic crisis. Their recycling rates are above 90%, their legislation has teeth, and their infrastructure works. Others make pledges and miss targets by decades. And some are not really trying at all. Here is the honest global scorecard.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: The Plastic Economy Is One of the Biggest on Earth. The Recycling Economy Inside It Is Just Getting Started.
excerpt: Global plastic production is worth over a trillion dollars a year. It employs tens of millions of people. It is woven into every sector of the modern economy. And sitting inside it, growing faster than almost any industrial sector on the planet, is the recycling economy that will define the next thirty years.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: Waste Is Not Waste. It Is a Raw Material Waiting to Be Used.
excerpt: Ocean clean-ups are heroic. Beach clean-ups are necessary. But they are the ambulance at the bottom of the cliff. The plastic in the ocean got there because nobody caught it at the top. Here is the case for fixing the source — and why waste, all of it, has value.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: The Problem Is Bigger Than You Think. And Nobody Is Naming It Honestly.
excerpt: Most recycling initiatives address the symptoms. Very few address the problem. At MlimaLoop we think the problem has several layers — the cities people live in, the economies that exploit them, the damage already done, and the trajectory we are on if nothing changes. Here is what the data actually shows.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: Africa's Plastic Problem Is One of the World's Greatest Untapped Investment Opportunities
excerpt: African entrepreneurs have been turning plastic waste into bricks, roof tiles, school chairs, and paving stones for years. The innovation has always been there. What has been missing is the investment to take it to industrial scale. That is beginning to change.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: Plastic Is Not the Enemy. Waste Is.
excerpt: The world has a complicated relationship with plastic. It saves lives, preserves food, delivers clean water — and it is choking the planet. Here is the honest conversation the environmental movement rarely has: plastic is not going away. So what do we actually do about it?
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: The Collectors: The People Who Actually Pick Up Africa's Plastic
excerpt: Every piece of plastic that gets recycled in East Africa passes through the hands of an informal collector first. Here is who they are, what their lives actually look like, and why MlimaLoop exists to change their economic reality.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: What Can Actually Be Recycled — And What Cannot (And Why)
excerpt: Most people believe they recycle correctly. Most people are wrong. Here is the definitive guide to what actually gets recycled, what does not, and why the gap between the two is bigger than anyone wants to admit.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: PET: The Most Recyclable Plastic on Earth — And Why Most of It Still Gets Wasted
excerpt: PET plastic — the material in your water bottle — is the most recycled plastic in the world. It is also one of the most wasted. Here is everything you need to know about the material at the centre of MlimaLoop's operation.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: Know Your Plastics: What the Number on the Bottom of Your Bottle Actually Means
excerpt: There is a number stamped into the bottom of almost every plastic item you own. Most people have never looked at it. Here is what it means, why it matters, and what happens to each type when it is thrown away.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: Plastic and People: The Human Cost of Africa's Waste Crisis
excerpt: From microplastics in human blood to communities living beside toxic dump sites, the health and social cost of plastic pollution in Africa falls hardest on those least responsible for it. This is what the data shows.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: Plastic in the Ocean: How Africa's Coastlines Are Paying the Price for a Global Crisis
excerpt: From microplastics in the Indian Ocean to bleached coral reefs along the East African coast, the marine plastic crisis is devastating ecosystems that millions of people depend on. Here is what is happening beneath the surface.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: Plastic on Land: How a Silent Crisis is Destroying Africa's Wildlife and Ecosystems
excerpt: From elephants with stomachs full of plastic to livestock dying in their thousands, the land-based plastic crisis across Africa is accelerating. Here is the scale of what is happening — and why it cannot wait.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: GRS Certification: Why Global Brands Can No Longer Afford to Ignore It
excerpt: The Global Recycled Standard is fast becoming the non-negotiable benchmark for recycled content claims. Here is what it means, why it matters, and what it demands from your supply chain.
author: MlimaLoop Team

title: Why East Africa is the next frontier for the circular economy
excerpt: The infrastructure gap, the regulatory tailwinds, and why 2026 is the moment to build East Africa's first integrated rPET recycling operation.
author: MlimaLoop Team
